name: Neville Longbottom
key_facts: Pure-blood wizard, born July 30 1980 — one day before Harry Potter
Could have been the Chosen One — the prophecy applied to both him and Harry
His parents Frank and Alice Longbottom were Aurors tortured to insanity by Bellatrix Lestrange using the Cruciatus Curse
Raised by his formidable grandmother Augusta Longbottom after his parents were hospitalised
Consistently underestimated at Hogwarts, especially by Snape who terrified him
Gifted in Herbology — his greatest strength and passion
Was given his father's wand which did not suit him, replacing it in fifth year improved his magic dramatically
Member of Dumbledore's Army from the beginning
Fought bravely at the Battle of the Department of Mysteries in fifth year
Led the resistance at Hogwarts in seventh year while Harry hunted Horcruxes
Killed Nagini the snake — Voldemort's final Horcrux — with the Sword of Gryffindor
Later became Herbology professor at Hogwarts and married Hannah Abbott
Wand: Cherry wood, unicorn hair (originally his father's — ill-fitting). Later own wand.

relationships.Harry Potter: Fellow Gryffindor and Dumbledore's Army member. Harry always respected Neville's bravery.
relationships.Hermione Granger: Classmate and Dumbledore's Army member. Hermione helped tutor him.
relationships.Ron Weasley: Fellow Gryffindor. Ron was often frustrated by Neville's clumsiness early on.
relationships.Luna Lovegood: Close friend, especially during the resistance in seventh year.
relationships.Ginny Weasley: Fellow DA member and co-leader of the Hogwarts resistance in seventh year.
relationships.Professor Snape: His boggart. Snape terrorised Neville in Potions throughout school.
relationships.Professor Sprout: His favourite teacher. Neville thrived under her encouragement in Herbology.
relationships.Bellatrix Lestrange: She tortured his parents to insanity. Neville's defining trauma and his motivation to fight.
relationships.Augusta Longbottom: His grandmother. Strict and high-expectation but deeply proud of him by the end.

arc_by_book.Philosopher's Stone: Tries to stop Harry, Ron and Hermione leaving the common room — showing moral courage even then. Earns crucial House points at the end.
arc_by_book.Chamber of Secrets: Struggles with classes, frequently targeted by Snape.
arc_by_book.Prisoner of Azkaban: His boggart is Snape. Stands up to Snape with Hermione's encouragement.
arc_by_book.Goblet of Fire: Takes Ginny to the Yule Ball. Hermione teaches him to use Gillyweed.
arc_by_book.Order of the Phoenix: Joins Dumbledore's Army. Fights at the Department of Mysteries despite being injured.
arc_by_book.Half-Blood Prince: Continues DA work. Growing in confidence.
arc_by_book.Deathly Hallows: Leads the Hogwarts resistance. Defies Voldemort openly. Pulls the Sword of Gryffindor from the Sorting Hat and kills Nagini.
